progress_summary: Gate for the worker entry-export fix. workerd (wrangler 4.98+, bumped in via #261) rejected the Worker entry module at startup because it had non-handler named exports (NONCE_PLACEHOLDER string, LABEL_MAP/PRIORITY_MAP objects) — so `wrangler dev`/deploy couldn't boot it. Extracted the CORS/security-header/CSP-nonce helpers → src/lib/http.js and the Linear feedback config → src/lib/feedback-config.js; src/index.js now exports ONLY its default handler (0 named exports). Updated the two tests that imported those symbols (feedback, csp-nonce) to import from the new modules. PASS — vitest 345/345 clean, worker build clean, and `wrangler dev` now boots the worker (verified /api/health + POST /mcp initialize over real HTTP; it previously crashed with 'Incorrect type for map entry NONCE_PLACEHOLDER'). Same workerd module validation runs at deploy, so the next wrangler deploy is unblocked too.
next_actions: Open a PR for this fix and land it before the next deploy (it unblocks wrangler deploy on main).
Still pending in the Codex effort: the platform-neutral copy scrub (index hero, compare, glossary, security, uses, skills/[id], Header/SearchPalette).

last_run.runtime: wrangler dev boots; /api/health + POST /mcp verified over HTTP
